source_alignment.subject: English
source_alignment.source_statement: Draw inferences such as inferring characters' feelings, thoughts and motives from their actions, and justify inferences with evidence.
source_alignment.derived_objective_note: This pack teaches inference as an evidence-linked reading process using highlight, reason and justify interactions.

objective.child_goal: I can make an inference and prove it with evidence.
objective.statement: Infer characters' feelings, thoughts and motives and justify them using precise evidence from a text.
misconceptions: Makes a plausible inference but selects no textual evidence.
Copies a quote without explaining how it proves the inference.
Uses personal opinion instead of evidence from the text.

vocabulary.child_explanation: An idea you work out using clues in the text.
vocabulary.audio_script: An inference is what you work out from clues.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: A clue thread connecting text words to a thought bubble.

vocabulary.child_explanation: Words or details from the text that support your idea.
vocabulary.audio_script: Evidence is the part of the text that proves your thinking.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: Highlighted words clipped to an inference card.

vocabulary.child_explanation: Explain why your evidence proves your answer.
vocabulary.audio_script: To justify, explain how the evidence supports your inference.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: Because arrows between evidence and answer.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Highlight one word that shows the character is worried.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Reactivate evidence finding before full inference.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Find a clue in the words, not just in your head.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: A short sentence with selectable words and a feeling meter.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Build an inference chain: clue, inference, because.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Introduce inference as a linked reasoning process.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: A strong inference links the clue to your answer.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Three linked cards labelled clue, inference and because.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: She hid the torn map behind her back. What might this suggest?
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Model how an action can suggest a motive or feeling.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Hiding the map is evidence. It suggests she may feel guilty or worried.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Text highlight connects to inference options.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Choose the best inference, then highlight the words that prove it.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Practise answer and evidence selection together.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Pick your inference, then prove it with text evidence.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: A short extract with answer cards and highlightable evidence.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Which because sentence best justifies the evidence?
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Check whether learners explain the evidence rather than only quote it.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: The best because sentence explains how the clue proves the inference.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Evidence and inference cards with three because bridges.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Teach Miko why a quote alone is not a full answer.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Use teach-back to repair quote-without-explanation responses.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: I found a quote. Why do I still need to explain it?
teaching_sequence.visual_model: The companion holds a quote card with a missing because bridge.

manipulatives.purpose: Make invisible reading reasoning visible by linking highlighted evidence to inferences and explanations.
manipulatives.interaction_notes: Learners highlight text, choose inference cards, build because bridges and submit short responses with evidence links.
manipulatives.accessibility_notes: Text size controls, audio reading, keyboard highlighting and low-sensory focus mode are required.

manipulatives.purpose: Strengthen justification by assembling and stress-testing a chain from inference through precise evidence to explanation.
manipulatives.interaction_notes: Learners choose between competing inferences, sequence claim-evidence-reason cards, identify an unsupported leap and revise the chain so every conclusion is warranted by the extract.
manipulatives.accessibility_notes: Cards have text roles, numbers and audio playback; keyboard controls, explicit move buttons and single-switch scanning replace drag-only ordering, with a static outline view and reduced-motion mode that updates cards instantly without paths, flips or transitions.

practice.interleaving_notes: Interleave feeling, motive and prediction inferences after evidence selection is reliable.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Infer feelings from actions, speech and description.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: short extract + target feeling + selectable evidence spans
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Passages must be original, age-appropriate and culturally inclusive.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Build clue-inference-because chains for character motives.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: action clue + inference options + because bridge
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Include plausible distractors that lack evidence.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Prompt learners to explain how selected evidence proves an inference.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: highlighted quote + sentence stem + rubric tags
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Marking should support multiple valid explanations.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Use evidence to justify predictions about what may happen next.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: extract ending + likely prediction + evidence card
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Avoid trick questions; all accepted predictions need textual support.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Provide short spaced-review inference checks across texts and question types.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: one-paragraph extract + one inference + one evidence action
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Keep daily warm-up extracts short and readable.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Compare plausible interpretations by relevance, directness and coverage of textual evidence.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: original extract + two interpretations + evidence columns + strength judgement
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Allow multiple defensible readings when each is justified, while distinguishing stronger from weaker support.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Explain how precise author wording or structure contributes to an inference.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: extract + author clue + neutral contrast + local meaning-effect choice
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Avoid guaranteed reader reactions and decontextualised technique spotting.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Judge whether evidence is sufficient for claims of different strength and repair overclaiming.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: claim + clue set + sufficient/limited judgement + calibrated revision
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Accept concise alternative judgements when certainty and evidence are accurately matched.

body.prompt: Highlight the words that best show Maya is worried.
body.text: Maya checked the corridor again and twisted the torn map between her fingers.

hints: Look for actions that show unease.
Which words suggest she cannot relax?
question_variants.explanation: Checking the corridor again and twisting the map suggest Maya is worried or nervous.

body.prompt: Which sentence best justifies the inference that Omar is trying to hide something?
body.evidence: Omar pushed the notebook under his coat when the teacher came in.
choices: This suggests he is hiding something because he moves the notebook out of sight when an adult arrives.
This shows Omar likes coats.
This proves the teacher is angry.
This suggests the notebook is heavy.

expected_answer.value: This suggests he is hiding something because he moves the notebook out of sight when an adult arrives.

hints: The best answer links the action to the inference.
Look for the sentence that explains why the evidence matters.
question_variants.explanation: The best justification explains how pushing the notebook out of sight supports the inference.

body.prompt: What can you infer about Lena's feelings? Use evidence.
body.text: Lena smiled at the medal, but her hand shook as she pinned it to her jacket.

hints: She smiles, but her hand shakes.
A strong answer explains both clue and feeling.
question_variants.explanation: A strong response may infer that Lena is proud but nervous, using the smile and shaking hand as evidence.

body.prompt: Inference case 1: what is the most supportable inference from this fiction extract?
body.text: Nia reached the hall door, stopped, and checked the folded invitation for the third time. When music spilled into the corridor, she smoothed her sleeve and stepped inside.
choices: The main point is only that this is fiction.
Nia feels uncertain but decides to enter
Nia hates everyone inside and will immediately leave
The extract supports no inference at all.
selected_evidence: checked the folded invitation for the third time
smoothed her sleeve and stepped inside

scaffold_routes.text: numbered extract lines and phrase choices
scaffold_routes.reduced_choice: compare two interpretations or clues before restoring all options
scaffold_routes.vocabulary: optional in-context glosses without defining the answer

investigation_mission.strategic_unlock: select a precise clue, state an inference, then explain the connection and certainty
investigation_mission.reward: add one evidence-thread card to the shared case board
investigation_mission.retry_message: That interpretation exposed a useful clue gap. Keep the extract open, revise one link, and try again when ready.

expected_answer.value: Nia feels uncertain but decides to enter

hints: Combine what the text states with what the actions, wording or details reasonably imply.
Choose cautious language that fits all the clues without turning an inference into a proven fact.
question_variants.explanation: Nia feels uncertain but decides to enter is supported by 'checked the folded invitation for the third time' and 'smoothed her sleeve and stepped inside'. The inference goes beyond literal recall but remains limited by the text. A different response remains reviewable when it cites relevant text and explains its reasoning without overclaiming.

feedback.correct: Case link supported. Nia feels uncertain but decides to enter is supported by 'checked the folded invitation for the third time' and 'smoothed her sleeve and stepped inside'. The inference goes beyond literal recall but remains limited by the text. A different response remains reviewable when it cites relevant text and explains its reasoning without overclaiming.
feedback.evidence_feedback: Check that the selected words directly address this inference rather than only the topic.
feedback.justification_feedback: Explain what the clue reveals; do not leave the quotation to speak for itself.
feedback.sufficiency_feedback: Match certainty to evidence and distinguish plausible, well-supported and proven.
feedback.misconception_repair: Highlight one action or wording clue, paraphrase it, then complete 'This suggests... because...' before selecting an inference.

body.prompt: Overclaim repair 32: which revision removes an unsupported certainty from 'the passage proves the harbour will be destroyed'?
body.text: The gulls folded inland, low over the fields. Beyond the harbour wall, the horizon had vanished into one dark band, and shopkeepers began lifting signs from the pavement.
choices: Replace 'the passage proves the harbour will be destroyed' with 'people and animals are responding to approaching severe weather' because the extract supports the cautious interpretation but not the absolute claim.
the passage proves the harbour will be destroyed
Any outcome is equally likely because this is an inference question.
A confident claim needs no quotation.
selected_evidence: shopkeepers began lifting signs from the pavement
The gulls folded inland

body.prompt: Prediction check 33: which prediction stays within the evidence?

the speaker will never see the traveller again

misconception_repairs.misconception: Makes a plausible inference but selects no textual evidence.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: Learner chooses an inference but leaves evidence blank or highlights unrelated text.
repair_sequence: Ask what clue made them think that.
Restrict highlight choices to relevant sentence spans.
Reconnect the selected clue to the inference card.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: Learner selects relevant evidence for a new inference.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Copies a quote without explaining how it proves the inference.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: Short response includes evidence but no because explanation.
repair_sequence: Offer sentence stems linking quote to inference.
Ask learner to complete the explanation.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: Learner writes or selects a because statement explaining the evidence.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Uses personal opinion instead of evidence from the text.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: Learner justifies with 'I think' statements unrelated to text clues.
repair_sequence: Separate personal opinion cards from text evidence cards.
Ask learner to choose a clue before choosing an inference.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: Learner grounds a new inference in text evidence.

adaptive_support.low_sensory: Use focus mode with minimal decorative motion and high-contrast text panels.
adaptive_support.reduced_motion: Use static clue threads and card states instead of animated lines.
adaptive_support.audio_first: Optional extract and sentence replay uses only ElevenLabs assets after human listening approval. Browser TTS is prohibited; when audio is unavailable, visible text, clause chunking, line focus and adult or partner reading routes remain complete.
adaptive_support.reading_support: Offer chunked extracts, line focus and vocabulary popovers.
adaptive_support.attention_support: Split tasks into one visible step: infer, highlight or justify.
adaptive_support.processing_support: Allow text reread, evidence review and draft before submit.
adaptive_support.confidence_support: Use partial credit language for good inference but missing evidence.
adaptive_support.eal_support: Preteach inference, evidence, justify, suggests, motive and because.

evidence.mastery_rule: Expected requires accurate inference with relevant evidence; secure requires explaining how evidence proves the inference across new texts.
evidence.parent_summary: Can infer what a character may feel or think and support the answer with evidence from the text.
evidence.teacher_evidence: Highlights precise textual evidence and justifies inference using a because explanation.
next_actions: Interleave inference with vocabulary-in-context and authorial choice questions.
Use short-response rubrics for teacher moderation.
Add longer passage sets once evidence precision is stable.

qa.notes: Depth-wave review bank reaches the 240-item pilot target with three preserved curated questions and 237 deterministic candidates covering inference, evidence selection, justification, competing interpretations, author clues, evidence sufficiency and overclaiming across original fiction, diary, news, information, biography, persuasion, nature writing, poetry, review, letter and historical extracts. Generated candidates include SEND/dyslexia scaffolds, supported non-drag interactions, rich clue-to-inference feedback, pressure-free investigation missions and optional ElevenLabs references requiring human listening approval; browser TTS is prohibited. Independent English, teacher, SEND, accessibility, safeguarding, audio and renderer review remains required before promotion.

accessibility_policy.no_timer_or_penalty: Learners are never timed for mastery and never lose earned progress for mistakes; incorrect attempts route to calm repair, replay and retry.
accessibility_policy.equivalent_response_routes: Every child-facing task must preserve an equivalent route through touch, keyboard, switch, eye-gaze, AAC, pointing, partner-mediated or adult-scribed response where appropriate.
accessibility_policy.audio_equivalent: Audio and read-aloud support must complement visible text, visuals and replay. Child-facing narration must use produced, human-listening-approved assets before release; browser TTS is not a teaching or phonics fallback.
accessibility_policy.reduced_motion: Reduced-motion and static alternatives must preserve the same curriculum evidence, feedback and reward meaning without relying on animation.
